name: Pragmatic Skills Pack
generated_from: skills/*/SKILL.md frontmatter plus shell-first installer, host adapter metadata, and project AGENTS.md maintenance metadata

user_contract.routing: AGENTS.md -> entry -> triage -> one mode -> support skills by phase trigger

skills.name: command-discovery
skills.description: Resolve install, test, lint, typecheck, build, and local-run commands from project evidence instead of hardcoding them.
skills.summary: Resolve install, test, lint, typecheck, build, and local-run commands from project evidence instead of hardcoding them.
triggers: A workflow needs a project command and no exact command is already known.
Verification, TDD, build, or local-run evidence is needed.
outputs: command map with command, cwd, source, confidence, and run policy

requires_approval_before: Mutating dependency actions when they may change lockfiles or environment state.
Long-running servers if they affect shared resources.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: delegation
skills.description: Use real subagents or explicit role-separated passes without pretending subagents exist.
skills.summary: Use real subagents or explicit role-separated passes without pretending subagents exist.
triggers: Strict Change benefits from second perspective.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: evidence-driven-execution
skills.description: Back every claim with actual evidence or label it as an assumption.
skills.summary: Back every claim with actual evidence or label it as an assumption.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: exploration
skills.description: Investigate, diagnose, compare options, or clarify requirements before editing.
skills.summary: Investigate, diagnose, compare options, or clarify requirements before editing.
triggers: Need to inspect project before deciding whether to edit.
outputs: next mode if implementation is needed

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: fast-patch
skills.description: Small, clear, low-risk edits with narrow verification.
skills.summary: Small, clear, low-risk edits with narrow verification.
triggers: Tiny localized edits with low blast radius and no high-risk trigger.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Final compact, factual, evidence-based response after changes, proposals, or reviews.
skills.summary: Final compact, factual, evidence-based response after changes, proposals, or reviews.
triggers: Before final response after file/code work.
Before final response after proposals or review findings.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: project-agents-md
skills.description: Create, update, or refactor project-specific AGENTS.md instructions while preserving PSP and other managed blocks.
skills.summary: Maintain the current repository's project-specific AGENTS.md, including generation, cleanup, refactoring, and evidence-based command sections.
triggers: User explicitly asks to create, write, generate, update, improve, migrate, or refactor AGENTS.md or repository agent instructions.
Passive detection finds no AGENTS.md in the current repository and a coding agent workflow is being used.
Passive detection finds AGENTS.md exists but contains only generic PSP-managed content and no project-specific guidance.
Existing AGENTS.md is stale, duplicated, contradictory, hard to scan, missing command evidence, or unsafe to use as-is.

safety.approval_required: Passive generation or major refactor requires explicit user confirmation before writing.
requires_approval_before: Creating AGENTS.md only because it was passively missing.
Removing or materially changing existing project-owned constraints.
Changing instructions that affect production, deployment, secrets, destructive operations, or safety gates.

active: User asks for AGENTS.md generation, update, cleanup, migration, or refactor.
passive: Current repository has AGENTS.md but it is PSP-only or lacks project-specific content.
activation.routing_note: Users provide normal tasks. The agent may route here automatically, but passive writes require asking the user first.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Review actual changed files, diff, patch, and behavior before calling Standard or Strict work complete.
skills.summary: Review actual changed files, diff, patch, and behavior before calling Standard or Strict work complete.
triggers: Standard Change with behavior changes, tests, multiple files, or non-tiny diff.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: safety-gates
skills.description: Require explicit approval before destructive, production-affecting, security-sensitive, or hard-to-reverse actions.
skills.summary: Require explicit approval before destructive, production-affecting, security-sensitive, or hard-to-reverse actions.
triggers: Any gated action is about to occur.

approval_required_for: runtime dependency changes with behavior/security impact

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.name: strict-change
skills.description: High-risk work with safety gates, traceability, rollback thinking, evidence, verification, and review.
skills.summary: High-risk work with safety gates, traceability, rollback thinking, evidence, verification, and review.

safety.approval_required: Before gated actions only; read-only inspection, local tests, and draft patches do not require approval.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

skills.summary: Choose the smallest safe primary mode and re-route when evidence changes.
triggers: Whenever risk, scope, ambiguity, or blast radius changes.

skills.name: using-pragmatic-skills
skills.description: Start here for any development task and load only the next relevant skill.
skills.summary: Start here for any development task and load only the next relevant skill.
triggers: Any task involving code, files, tests, debugging, review, or project decisions.

safety.approval_required: Only for safety-gated actions, never just to continue ordinary low-risk work.

skills.name: verification
skills.description: Choose and run the right level of validation before claiming completion.
skills.summary: Choose and run the right level of validation before claiming completion.
triggers: Before reporting checks as passed or failed.

skills.description: Use git worktree isolation only when it materially reduces risk.
skills.summary: Use git worktree isolation only when it materially reduces risk.
triggers: Dirty tree with unrelated user changes.

skills.description: Create executable, verifiable plans for non-trivial implementation.
skills.summary: Create executable, verifiable plans for non-trivial implementation.
triggers: More than one file or step.

activation.routing_note: Users provide tasks; agents route from AGENTS.md through triage and phase triggers. Users do not manually invoke individual skills.

not_managed: files outside the PSP managed AGENTS.md block

upgrade_policy.default_command: The default install command is idempotent: it installs PSP when absent and upgrades PSP when present.
upgrade_policy.agents_md: Replace only the PSP managed block when present; append the block to project-owned AGENTS.md.
upgrade_policy.skills_and_reference: Overwrite only PSP-managed files that are unchanged since the last install; write conflicts to .psp/conflicts unless --force is used.
upgrade_policy.removed_files: Delete only files that still match the previous PSP-installed checksum.

tooling.upgrade_policy: Safe by default: overwrite only managed unmodified files, back up replaced files, and report conflicts instead of clobbering user edits.

host_adapters.duplicate_name_policy: Do not install the same native skill name into multiple locations that a host scans. Claude uses psp-claude-entry while .agents uses using-pragmatic-skills.

host_compatibility.strategy: single canonical workflow plus thin host adapters

active_triggers: add or improve repository agent instructions
passive_triggers: AGENTS.md exists but only contains generic PSP-managed content

ownership.skill: project-owned AGENTS.md content outside managed blocks
